debate_rounds.title: Chair Initial Assessment
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 6 addresses two remaining gaps. The _validate_frontmatter function validates published frontmatter against SSG requirements for nextjs/hugo/astro — checking required fields, delimiters, date format, slug format, and description length. This partially addresses the 'no end-to-end test verifying frontmatter renders in real SSG' gap. The _suggest_internal_links function provides corpus-based internal link suggestions, addressing the 'no automated internal link suggestion' gap. Frontmatter validation is integrated into cmd_publish and link suggestions into cmd_draft. Test coverage grew from 105 to 119 (+14). Both additions are meaningful depth and effectiveness improvements.

experts.rationale: Breadth: SSG frontmatter validation and internal link suggestion are two new capability areas. Depth: Frontmatter validation checks 7 aspects per platform (required fields, delimiters, date, slug, description). Link suggestion uses keyword overlap scoring. Thickness: 119 tests. The 7 frontmatter validation tests cover valid/invalid cases per platform. 4 link suggestion tests cover corpus, empty, nonexistent, and relevance sorting. Effectiveness: Frontmatter validation in cmd_publish catches SSG incompatibilities before commit. Link suggestions in cmd_draft help authors add relevant internal links.

Breadth: SSG frontmatter validation and corpus-based link suggestion are genuine additions. The frontmatter validation catches real problems — missing fields, bad slugs, wrong date formats — that would cause SSG build failures. Depth: The validation covers nextjs/hugo/astro with platform-specific required field lists. The link suggestion uses title-keyword overlap scoring, which is practical. Thickness: 119 tests with good coverage of validation edge cases and link suggestion scenarios. Effectiveness: Frontmatter validation prevents deployment failures. Link suggestions reduce manual internal link research.

Breadth: Both features close real gaps. Frontmatter validation addresses the SSG rendering concern. Link suggestion addresses the manual internal link gap. Depth: The frontmatter validation is thorough — it checks delimiters, date format, slug format, and description length. The link suggestion scores articles by keyword overlap, which is a practical relevance metric. Thickness: 119 tests. The frontmatter validation tests are particularly good — they cover valid cases, missing fields, space-in-slug, invalid date, and platform-specific fields like pubDate. Effectiveness: Publishing with frontmatter validation catches SSG issues pre-commit. Link suggestions make the draft→publish pipeline more self-contained.

Breadth: Frontmatter validation and link suggestion are meaningful breadth additions. The SSG-specific validation for nextjs/hugo/astro addresses a real deployment concern. Depth: The frontmatter validation catches real SSG incompatibilities. The link suggestion engine uses keyword overlap, which is simple but effective. The integration into cmd_publish and cmd_draft shows proper pipeline thinking. Thickness: 119 tests with good platform-specific validation coverage. Effectiveness: Frontmatter validation in the publish flow catches issues before they reach the SSG build.

Breadth: Frontmatter validation and link suggestion are real additions. But frontmatter validation doesn't test rendering in an actual SSG — it checks field presence and format, which is necessary but not sufficient for rendering. Depth: The validation checks are solid for field presence and format. The link suggestion is keyword matching, not semantic relevance. Thickness: 119 tests. Good coverage of validation edge cases. Effectiveness: The validation catches format issues but not rendering issues. A frontmatter with all required fields can still fail to render if the values are semantically wrong.

challenges.challenge: Lily scores effectiveness at 22, but frontmatter validation checks field presence, not rendering. A slug with all ASCII characters can still fail if the SSG expects a specific slug format. The link suggestion counts keyword overlaps, which is a weak relevance signal. I propose reducing from 22 to 21.

debate_rounds.title: Advocate Supports

supports.support: The 119 tests now include 7 frontmatter validation tests covering valid/invalid cases across nextjs, hugo, astro. The 4 link suggestion tests cover corpus-based, empty, nonexistent, and relevance-sorted scenarios. The end-to-end pipeline tests (draft→score→validate→publish, draft→schema, draft→editorial-review) verify real pipeline flows. 119 tests is strong for this project scope.

debate_rounds.statement: The test coverage is substantive. 119 tests with platform-specific validation, corpus-based link suggestion, and end-to-end pipeline flows.

debate_rounds.title: Chair Synthesis
debate_rounds.statement: Iteration 6 advances from 83 to 87. SSG frontmatter validation partially addresses the rendering concern — it validates field presence and format, which catches the most common SSG build failures. Internal link suggestion provides corpus-aware link recommendations. Remaining gaps: (1) no actual SSG build test (validation checks format, not rendering), (2) scoring model uses regex heuristics, not validated against real search data, (3) draft generates scaffolding, not full content. The project is approaching diminishing returns on the easier gaps.

gaps: No actual SSG build test (frontmatter validation checks format, not rendering in real SSG)
Scoring model uses regex heuristics, not validated against real search performance data
Draft generates scaffolding, not full article content
No multi-language article generation in cmd_draft
No semantic relevance scoring for internal link suggestions (uses keyword overlap only)
